description: Solve and validate explicit magnetic attraction or repulsion using a finite-range force adapter, source-relative trajectory evidence, force traces, and UE multimodal replay.
physical_assumptions: Magnetic motion must be caused by a structured magnetic field label, not by hidden animation.
A magnetic source and magnetized body must have stable object ids.
Attraction decreases radial distance to the source; repulsion increases radial distance from the source.
The radial displacement must stay within the declared expected range.
Every trajectory frame and subject state must retain the declared solver provenance.
The subject must settle below the declared terminal-speed threshold during the post-event tail.
required_assets: magnetic source or analytic field proxy
verifier_rules: Expected magnetic mode is attract or repel.
Magnetized subject trajectory has at least two frames.
Attract mode moves radially toward the source.
Repel mode moves radially away from the source.
Radial displacement stays within expected bounds.
Trajectory and object-state provenance equal required_trajectory_source.
Final subject speed stays below maximum_final_speed_m_s.
repair_suggestions: Bind the magnetic source and subject as physics-critical objects or analytic proxies.
Export trajectory frames with source-relative positions, velocities, solver source, and per-frame force vectors.
Use negative cases for missing magnetic labels, no magnetic motion, and wrong radial direction.
